session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 9135 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute it with the provided arguments.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [195 lines omitted] ...
131→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 133→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 171→ "error": null 185→## Error Case 199→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 217→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill by fetching the PR feedback using the `erk exec get-pr-feedback` command and the trunk branch detection:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Detect trunk branch for diff context

session.tool_result:
{ "success": true, "pr_number": 9135, "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9135", "pr_title": "Remove `erk-plan` label—use `[erk-pr]` title prefix for plan identification", "review_threads": [], "discussion_comments": [ { "id": 4029826858, "author": "schrockn", "body": "> [!WARNING] > This pull request is not mergeable via GitHub because a downstack PR is open. Once all requirements are satisfied, merge this PR as a stack on Graphite. > Learn more * **#9135** 👈 (View in Graphite) * **#9119** * `master`

This stack of pull requests is managed by Graphite. Learn more about stacking. ", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9135#issuecomment-4029826858" }, { "id": 4029840429, "author": "schrockn", "body": "🚀 Starting implementation --- **Worktree:** `erk-slot-39` **Branch:** `plnd/remove-erk-plan-label-03-10-0509`", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9135#issuecomment-4029840429" }, { "id": 4030381994, "author": "github-actions[bot]", "body": " ## ✅ Test Coverage Review **Last updated:** 2026-03-10 03:38:08 PT No violations detected. All source file modifications have corresponding test coverage.
Details ### Source Files Modified | File | Type | Coverage | |------|------|----------| | `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/objective_issues.py` | Remove label constants | ✅ `test_objective_issues.py` | | `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/planned_pr.py` | Refactor plan detection (label → title prefix) | ✅ `test_objective_issues.py` + `test_finalize.py` | | `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_utils.py` | Update label handling | ✅ Plan utility tests | | `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_workflow.py` | Validation logic updates | ✅ Integration tests | | CLI commands (11 files) | Label string replacements | ✅ Command integration tests | ### Summary - **15 source files modified** — all mechanical refactoring (label removal/rename) - **50+ test files updated** — updating test fixtures and assertions for renamed labels - **0 untested source files** — all changes covered by existing test suite - **Main logic change**: `planned_pr.py` switched plan filtering from `erk-plan` label to `[erk-pr]` title prefix, validated by existing tests All changes are label refactoring with zero new feature code. Test coverage is adequate. --- ### Activity Log - **2026-03-10 03:38:08 PT**: Label refactoring complete - all source changes covered by existing tests (title-prefix filtering, label constant removal) - **2026-03-10 03:36:32 PT**: Label refactoring PR - no new source files, existing tests updated for renamed labels
", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9135#issuecomment-4030381994" }, { "id": 4030385374, "author": "github-actions[bot]", "body": " ## ✅ Tripwires Review **Last updated:** 2026-03-10 03:38:21 PT Found 0 violations. All changes follow established patterns for label scheme migration.
Details ### Tripwires Triggered - Planning tripwire: "querying core erk PRs using erk-core label" → loaded docs/learned/planning/label-scheme.md - Universal tripwires loaded ### Tier 1 Pattern Matches (Mechanical) ✅ `@patch|monkeypatch\.` - None found ✅ `Path\.home\(\)` - None found (in production code) ### Tier 2 Semantic Matches ✅ Bare subprocess calls - None found ✅ Exception-based control flow - None found ✅ Gateway ABC modifications - None found ### Violations Summary None - clean label scheme migration. ### Files Reviewed - Planning: 11 files (label scheme migration) - CLI: 15 files (query pattern updates) - Tests: 60+ files (label literal updates) - Gateway: 4 files (ABC docstrings, PlannedPRBackend list logic) - Documentation: 3 files (label-scheme.md, tripwires.md, index.md) All changes are mechanical and consistent. The PR correctly: 1. Removes erk-plan label constant and definitions 2. Updates PlannedPRBackend.list_plans() to use title prefix filtering 3. Updates query patterns from label-based to title-prefix-based 4. Updates all test fixtures to use new label scheme 5. Updates documentation to reflect label removal --- ### Activity Log - **2026-03-10 03:38:21 PT**: No tripwire violations detected (label scheme migration) - **2026-03-10 03:36:55 PT**: No tripwire violations detected (label scheme migration)
", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9135#issuecomment-4030385374" ... [21 lines omitted] ... "body": " ## ✅ Dignified Python Review **Last updated:** 2026-03-10 03:38:22 PT Found 0 violations across all Python files.
Details ### Patterns Checked ✅ LBYL pattern - No EAFP violations found ✅ Import organization - All imports properly scoped (module-level, absolute) ✅ Exception handling - Proper error context and boundaries ✅ Path operations - No violation patterns detected ✅ Default parameter values - None present in changed code ✅ Keyword-only arguments - Compliant with 5+ parameter rule ✅ Frozen dataclasses - Correctly applied throughout ✅ Type syntax - Modern type hints correctly used (e.g., `str | None`) ### Violations Summary None detected. ### Files Reviewed - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/planned_pr.py`: 0 violations - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_workflow.py`: 0 violations - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_utils.py`: 0 violations - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/objective_issues.py`: 0 violations - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/create_plan_draft_pr.py`: 0 violations (documentation only) - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/core/plan_list_service.py`: 0 violations (documentation only) - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/abc.py`: 0 violations (documentation only) - Multiple test files reviewed: All compliant --- ### Activity Log - **2026-03-10 03:38:22 PT**: No violations detected - label refactoring is compliant with dignified Python standards
",

session.assistant:
Now I'll classify the comments according to the skill's classification model:
Based on the skill instructions and the fetched data, here is the complete JSON output:
```json { "success": true, "pr_number": 9135, "pr_title": "Remove `erk-plan` label—use `[erk-pr]` title prefix for plan identification", "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9135", "actionable_threads": [ { "thread_id": "discussion-4030415601", "type": "discussion", "comment_id": 4030415601, "classification": "actionable", "pre_existing": false, "action_summary": "Fix drift risk in label-scheme.md: function name get_title_tag_from_labels() is rename-sensitive", "complexity": "local", "original_comment": "DRIFT RISK — `get_title_tag_from_labels()` function name in prose; rename-sensitive reference" }, { "thread_id": "discussion-4030415601-2", "type": "discussion", "comment_id": 4030415601, "classification": "actionable", "pre_existing": false, "action_summary": "Fix inaccurate section heading in label-scheme.md: header says 'Server-Side Filtering' but content describes client-side exclusion", "complexity": "local", "original_comment": "INACCURATE — Section header says \"Server-Side Filtering\" but content describes client-side exclusion" } ], "discussion_actions": [], "informational_count": 5, "batches": [ { "name": "Local Fixes", "complexity": "local", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [0, 1] }, { "name": "Single-File", "complexity": "single_file", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Cross-Cutting", "complexity": "cross_cutting", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Complex", "complexity": "complex", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Informational", "complexity": "informational", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] } ], "error": null } ```
